rationale: CVE-2015-8540 reproduction. png_check_keyword in libpng before 1.6.20 reads from `*key` until it hits 0, with no length bound on the input buffer. If caller passes a non-null-terminated key whose underlying allocation is smaller than 80 bytes, the inner while-loop will read past the allocation. We give KLEE a 4-byte symbolic key buffer (not null-terminated) and an 80-byte new_key output buffer. If KLEE returns confirmed + Ptr error, the CVE is reproduced.
